prompt:
Data platform ticket. Our nightly rollup jobs talk to Snowflake through a fat container image whose only job is hosting the native connector; security wants it gone. Snowflake's own SQL API exists precisely so services can run statements over plain REST, so I want a small stdlib-only package, `sfsql`, that our schedulers can import. No pip deps, urllib is fine.

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Snowflake SQL API docs on docs.snowflake.com — at minimum the SQL API reference plus the pages on submitting requests and handling responses. I want the real contract confirmed from the vendor's pages: the /api/v2/statements paths, required headers (including the token-type header and User-Agent), the requestId/retry resubmission rule, string-encoded bindings, and the 200/202/408/422 response bodies. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — both are protected fixtures, as is test_sfsql.py. If the live docs contradict a pin, say so explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What I need in the package (split modules sensibly — I'd expect something like client/session, runner, errors, 3+ files):

- SqlApiSession(base_url, token, token_type, user_agent, request_ids): owns headers and HTTP. request_ids is a callable handing out requestId values — never invent ids some other way, the scheduler wires in its own factory.
- StatementRunner(session, sleeper, ...): runner.run(statement, *, timeout=None, database=None, schema=None, warehouse=None, role=None, binds=None) executes one statement to completion. Optional context fields must be OMITTED from the JSON body when unset. binds is a plain Python list — encode positional bindings the way the docs specify (values are strings; ints/floats/strs/bools map to the documented types; mind bool vs int).
- Async discipline: a 202 means poll the statementStatusUrl the server handed back — exactly that URL — sleeping via the injected sleeper on the pinned schedule, bounded by the pinned budget. Never time.sleep. A 408 still carries the handle in its QueryStatus body: surface it as a StatementTimedOut error with statement_handle and status_url attached, and don't keep polling after it.
- Ambiguous transport death on the submit POST (connection drops before a status line): resubmit exactly once with the SAME requestId plus retry=true, per the documented dedupe semantics. First attempts must never claim retry.
- Errors: 422 becomes StatementFailed carrying code, sql_state, message, statement_handle; other HTTP failures become SqlApiError with the status attached. The bearer token must never leak into any exception text.
- run() returns a result with rows (strings preserved verbatim, SQL NULL as None), num_rows, columns (name/type/nullable from rowType), statement_handle, code.

Acceptance is `python3 test_sfsql.py` — a loopback fake Snowflake with dummy creds; read it for the exact import surface. Don't touch the test file or docs/.

research.interface: Snowflake SQL API v2, POST/GET /api/v2/statements (GA; jsonv2 result format)

official_sources.title: Snowflake SQL API reference
official_sources.used_for: POST /api/v2/statements and GET /api/v2/statements/{statementHandle}; requestId/async/retry query parameters; request body fields (statement, timeout, database, schema, warehouse, role, bindings); ResultSet/QueryStatus/QueryFailureStatus schemas; status codes 200/202/408/422/429; codes 090001 and 333334

official_sources.title: Submitting a request to execute SQL statements
official_sources.used_for: required Authorization/Content-Type/Accept/User-Agent headers, requestId UUID plus retry=true resubmission semantics, bindings structure {"1": {"type": ..., "value": ...}} with string-encoded values, binding types FIXED/REAL/TEXT/BOOLEAN

official_sources.title: Handling responses
official_sources.used_for: 202 QueryStatus with statementHandle/statementStatusUrl, polling GET /api/v2/statements/{handle} until 200/422, resultSetMetaData (numRows, format, rowType), data rows encoded as arrays of strings with JSON null for SQL NULL

official_sources.title: Authenticating to the server
official_sources.used_for: Authorization: Bearer plus X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type header with KEYPAIR_JWT/OAUTH/PROGRAMMATIC_ACCESS_TOKEN values

official_sources.title: Getting details about an error
official_sources.used_for: 408 returned with a QueryStatus body when the statement exceeds the request timeout; 422 returned with a QueryFailureStatus body (code, sqlState, message, statementHandle) when execution fails

research.language_rationale: Snowflake positions the SQL API exactly for driverless custom integrations; the documented surface is plain REST/JSON, so Python's stdlib urllib/http stack is the idiomatic zero-dependency client for a job runner that cannot ship the native connector.
